task_list.name: BaSiC: Calculate and apply illumination correction for plate

autosegment.title: Autosegment
autosegment.description: When not False, automatically segment the image before fitting. When True, threshold_otsu from scikit-image is used and the brighter pixels are taken.When a callable is given, it is used as the segmentation function.

autosegment_margin.title: Autosegment Margin
autosegment_margin.description: Margin of the segmentation mask to the thresholded region.

max_iterations.title: Max Iterations
max_iterations.description: Maximum number of iterations for single optimization.

max_mu_coef.title: Max Mu Coef
max_mu_coef.description: Maximum allowed value of mu, divided by the initial value.

max_reweight_iterations_baseline.title: Max Reweight Iterations Baseline
max_reweight_iterations_baseline.description: Maximum number of reweighting iterations for baseline.

max_workers.title: Max Workers
max_workers.description: Maximum number of threads used for processing.

reweighting_tol.title: Reweighting Tol
reweighting_tol.description: Reweighting tolerance in mean absolute difference of images.

sort_intensity.title: Sort Intensity
sort_intensity.description: Whether or not to sort the intensities of the image.

sparse_cost_darkfield.title: Sparse Cost Darkfield
sparse_cost_darkfield.description: Weight of the darkfield sparse term in the Lagrangian.

working_size.title: Working Size
working_size.description: Size for running computations. None means no rescaling.

n_images_sampled.title: N Images Sampled
n_images_sampled.description: Number of images to sample for illumination correction. If there are less images available than n_images, all available images will be used.

smoothness_flatfield.title: Smoothness Flatfield
smoothness_flatfield.description: Smoothing parameter for flatfield. (Weight of the flatfield term in the Lagrangian.)

smoothness_darkfield.title: Smoothness Darkfield
smoothness_darkfield.description: Smoothing parameter for darkfield. (Weight of the darkfield term in the Lagrangian.)

random_seed.title: Random Seed
random_seed.description: Integer random seed to initialize random number generator. None will result in non-reproducibel outputs.

overwrite_illumination_profiles.title: Overwrite Illumination Profiles
overwrite_illumination_profiles.description: If True, overwrite existing illumination profiles of the same name. If False, an error is raised if illumination profiles already exist.

overwrite_input_image.title: Overwrite Input Image
overwrite_input_image.description: If True, overwrite the input image. If False, create a new well sub-group to store the corrected image.

new_well_subgroup_suffix.title: New Well Subgroup Suffix
new_well_subgroup_suffix.description: Suffix to add to original well sub-group name. Only used if overwrite_input_image is False.

subtract_median_baseline.title: Subtract Median Baseline
subtract_median_baseline.description: If True, do a background subtraction by subtracting the median of all baseline values from the corrected image.

zarr_urls.title: Zarr Urls
zarr_urls.description: List of paths or urls to the individual OME-Zarr images to be processed. (Standard argument for Fractal tasks, managed by Fractal server).

zarr_dir.title: Zarr Dir
zarr_dir.description: Profiles will be saved in {zarr_dir}/{illumination_profiles_folder_name} (Standard argument for Fractal tasks, managed by Fractal server).

illumination_profiles_folder_name.title: Illumination Profiles Folder Name
illumination_profiles_folder_name.description: Name of folder to save illumination profiles in. The folder will be created inside zarr_dir.

core_basic_parameters.title: Core Basic Parameters
core_basic_parameters.description: Core parameters for BaSiC illumination correction.

advanced_basic_parameters.title: Advanced Basic Parameters
advanced_basic_parameters.description: Advanced parameters for BaSiC illumination correction. See https://basicpy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html

zarr_url.title: Zarr Url
zarr_url.description: Absolute path to the OME-Zarr image. (standard argument for Fractal tasks, managed by Fractal server).

init_args.title: Init Args
init_args.description: Initialization arguments from the init task.

task_list.docs_info:
## basic_correct_illumination_plate_init
Calculate illumination profiles and correct channels using BaSiC.

See https://basicpy.readthedocs.io for more information on BaSiC.
This task calculates illumination correction profiles based on a random
sample of FOVs of the entire plate for each channel. It stores the
calculated illumination profiles in a specified folder and corrects each
image in the plate using these profiles.
NOTE: This assumes that all FOVs in the plate have the same dimensions.
## basic_apply_illumination_profile
Applies illumination correction to the OME-Zarr.

Uses illumination profiles calculated using the 'BaSiC: Calculate
illumination profile for plate' task.

task_list.name: Merge acquisitions along channel axis

zarr_urls.title: Zarr Urls
zarr_urls.description: List of paths or urls to the individual OME-Zarr images to be processed. (Standard argument for Fractal tasks, managed by Fractal server).

zarr_dir.title: Zarr Dir
zarr_dir.description: Profiles will be saved in {zarr_dir}/{illumination_profiles_folder_name} (Standard argument for Fractal tasks, managed by Fractal server).

acquisitions_to_combine.title: Acquisitions To Combine
acquisitions_to_combine.description: Optional: List of acquisition IDs to combine. If left empty, all acquisitions found in the plate will be combined.

keep_individual_acquisitions.title: Keep Individual Acquisitions
keep_individual_acquisitions.description: If True, keep the individual acquisitions and add combined acquisition. If False, delete them and only keep the combined acquisition.

zarr_url.title: Zarr Url
zarr_url.description: Absolute path to the new OME-Zarr image.

init_args.title: Init Args
init_args.description: Initialization arguments from the init task.

task_list.docs_info:
## combine_acquisitions_init
Combine multiple acquisitions of a plate into a single acquisition.

If there are multiple acquisitions for a given well, this task will
combine them into a single acquisition by concatenating the channels.

Tables will be copied from the first acquisition. Label images are not
handled in this version.
## combine_acquisitions_parallel
Combine multiple acquisitions into a single acquisition.

task_list.name: Delete labels from OME-Zarr image

zarr_url.title: Zarr Url
zarr_url.description: Absolute path to the OME-Zarr image. (standard argument for Fractal tasks, managed by Fractal server).

labels_to_delete.title: Labels To Delete
labels_to_delete.description: Add names of labels to delete. If left empty, all labels will be deleted.

task_list.docs_info:
## delete_labels
Delete labels from an OME-Zarr image.

task_list.name: Update display range

zarr_url.title: Zarr Url
zarr_url.description: Absolute path to the OME-Zarr image. (standard argument for Fractal tasks, managed by Fractal server).

pyramid_level.title: Pyramid Level
pyramid_level.description: Resolution level to calculate percentiles on. Choose `0` for full resolution.

percentiles.title: Percentiles
percentiles.description: lower and upper percentiles to calculate e.g. [0.5, 99.5]

task_list.docs_info:
## update_display_range
Update display range of image based on percentiles. (Saved in omero metadata).

task_list.name: Expand segmentation

zarr_url.title: Zarr Url
zarr_url.description: Path or url to the individual OME-Zarr image to be processed. (standard argument for Fractal tasks, managed by Fractal server).

input_ROI_table.title: Input Roi Table
input_ROI_table.description: Name of the ROI table over which the task loops to apply segmentation. Examples: `FOV_ROI_table` => loop over the field of views, `organoid_ROI_table` => loop over the organoid ROI table (generated by another task), `well_ROI_table` => process the whole well as one image.

input_label_name.title: Input Label Name
input_label_name.description: Name of the input label image to be expanded (e.g. `"nuclei"`).

expansion_distance.title: Expansion Distance
expansion_distance.description: Distance by which the labels are expanded, in pixels at level 0.

save_union.title: Save Union
save_union.description: If `True`, save the union of the original and expanded labels. (corresponds to e.g. the intire cell)

union_output_label_name.title: Union Output Label Name
union_output_label_name.description: Name of the output label image for the union (e.g. `cells`).

save_difference.title: Save Difference
save_difference.description: If `True`, save the difference between the original and expanded labels. (corresponds to e.g. the cytoplasm)

difference_output_label_name.title: Difference Output Label Name
difference_output_label_name.description: Name of the output label image for the difference (e.g. `cytoplasms`).

overwrite_existing_label.title: Overwrite Existing Label
overwrite_existing_label.description: If `True`, overwrite the created labels, if they already exist.

task_list.docs_info:
## expand_segmentation
Expand the labels on the ROIs of a single OME-Zarr image.

task_list.name: Histograms: Calculate channel-histograms for each image

zarr_url.title: Zarr Url
zarr_url.description: Absolute path to the OME-Zarr image. (standard argument for Fractal tasks, managed by Fractal server).

pyramid_level.title: Pyramid Level
pyramid_level.description: Resolution level to calculate histograms on. (E.g. `0` for full resolution).

input_ROI_table.title: Input Roi Table
input_ROI_table.description: Name of the ROI table over which the task loops. If left empty, the whole image is used.

bin_width.title: Bin Width
bin_width.description: Width of the histogram bins. A bin-width of 1 is suitable for integer-valued images (e.g. 8-bit or 16-bit images).

update_display_range.title: Update Display Range
update_display_range.description: If True, update the display range of the image. (Saved in the omero metadata of the zarr file).

display_range_percentiles.title: Display Range Percentiles
display_range_percentiles.description: Percentiles (e.g. [0.5, 99.5]) to use for display range calculation. (Only used if update_display_range is True).

histogram_name.title: Histogram Name
histogram_name.description: Name of the output histogram table.

overwrite.title: Overwrite
overwrite.description: If True, overwrite existing histogram table.

task_list.docs_info:
## histogram_calculate
Calculate channel histograms of image.

Calculates intensity histograms for each channel of the image,
by looping over ROIs defined in the input ROI table. The resulting
histograms are saved in a new table in the OME-Zarr.

task_list.name: Histograms: Aggregate plate-histograms

zarr_urls.title: Zarr Urls
zarr_urls.description: List of paths or urls to the individual OME-Zarr images to be processed. (Standard argument for Fractal tasks, managed by Fractal server).

zarr_dir.title: Zarr Dir
zarr_dir.description: Not used for this task. (Standard argument for Fractal tasks, managed by Fractal server).

histogram_input_name.title: Histogram Input Name
histogram_input_name.description: Table name of the histogram table to be combined. (Output of the "Histograms: Calculate channel-histograms for each image" task).

histogram_output_name.title: Histogram Output Name
histogram_output_name.description: Table name of the output combined histogram.

update_display_range.title: Update Display Range
update_display_range.description: If True, update the display range of the images. (Saved in the omero metadata of the zarr file).

display_range_percentiles.title: Display Range Percentiles
display_range_percentiles.description: Percentiles (e.g. [0.5, 99.5]) to use for display range calculation. (Only used if update_display_range is True).

overwrite.title: Overwrite
overwrite.description: If True, overwrite existing histogram table.

task_list.docs_info:
## histogram_aggregate_plate
Combine all histograms in a plate and write to table in each image.

All channel intensity histograms in each image are loaded and combined. The
resulting combined histograms are then saved in a new table in each image.

ONE FIRST NEEDS TO RUN THE "Histograms: Calculate channel-histograms for each image"
TASK!

input_label_name.title: Input Label Name
input_label_name.description: Name of the label to be used for measurement.

output_table_name.title: Output Table Name
output_table_name.description: Name of corresponding output feature table.

use_all_channels.title: Use All Channels
use_all_channels.description: If True, all channels are selected.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: Specifies how to interpret the identifier. Can be "label", "wavelength_id", or "index" (must be an integer).

identifiers.title: Identifiers
identifiers.description: Unique identifier for the channel. This can be a channel label, wavelength ID, or index.

zarr_url.title: Zarr Url
zarr_url.description: Path or url to the individual OME-Zarr image to be processed. (standard argument for Fractal tasks, managed by Fractal server).

structure_props.title: Structure Props
structure_props.description: List of regionprops structure properties to measure.

intensity_props.title: Intensity Props
intensity_props.description: List of regionprops intensity properties to measure.

roi_table.title: Roi Table
roi_table.description: ROI table name to iterate over (e.g 'FOV_ROI_table'). If left empty, measure over whole image.

append_to_table.title: Append To Table
append_to_table.description: If True, append new measurements to existing table. If False, overwrite existing table.

task_list.name: Measure parent label

zarr_url.title: Zarr Url
zarr_url.description: Path or url to the individual OME-Zarr image to be processed. (standard argument for Fractal tasks, managed by Fractal server).

input_label_name.title: Input Label Name
input_label_name.description: Name of the label that contains the seeds. Needs to exist in OME-Zarr file.

parent_label_names.title: Parent Label Names
parent_label_names.description: Names of the parent labels to assign to.

pyramid_level.title: Pyramid Level
pyramid_level.description: Resolution level of the label image to use for calculations. Choose `0` for full resolution. Only tested for 2D images at level 0.

roi_table.title: Roi Table
roi_table.description: ROI table name to iterate over (e.g 'FOV_ROI_table'). If left empty, measure over whole image.

append_to_table.title: Append To Table
append_to_table.description: If True, append new measurements to existing table.

task_list.docs_info:
## measure_parent_label
Assign label to parent label.

Takes a label image and a parent label image and assigns each label to a
parent label based on maximum overlap. Writes results to a feature table.

task_list.name: Measure shortest distance to label

zarr_url.title: Zarr Url
zarr_url.description: Path or url to the individual OME-Zarr image to be processed. (standard argument for Fractal tasks, managed by Fractal server).

input_label_name.title: Input Label Name
input_label_name.description: Name of the label that contains the seeds. Needs to exist in OME-Zarr file.

target_label_names.title: Target Label Names
target_label_names.description: Names of the target labels to measure distance to.

pyramid_level.title: Pyramid Level
pyramid_level.description: Resolution level of the label image to use for calculations. Choose `0` for full resolution. Only tested for 2D images at level 0.

roi_table_name.title: Roi Table Name
roi_table_name.description: Name of the ROI table to iterate over.

append.title: Append
append.description: If True, append new measurements to existing table.

overwrite.title: Overwrite
overwrite.description: Only used if append is False. If True, overwrite existing table. If False, raise error if table already exists.

task_list.docs_info:
## measure_shortest_distance
Measure shortest distance of labels to target labels.

Takes a label image and a target label image and calculates the shortest
distance from each label to the nearest target label. Writes results to a
feature table.

task_list.name: Cellpose segmentation, simple

CustomNormalizer.description: Validator to handle different image normalization scenarios.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: Normalization mode. 'default': Data is normalized so 0.0=1st percentile and 1.0=99th percentile of image intensities. 'no_normalization': No normalization is applied. 'custom': You can either provide your own rescaling percentiles or fixed rescaling upper and lower bound integers. 'omero': The "start" and "end" values from the omero channels in the zarr file are used for upper and lower bounds. 'histogram': A precalculated histogram is used for normalization. Percentiles need to be provided.

lower_percentile.title: Lower Percentile
lower_percentile.description: Specify a custom lower-bound percentile for rescaling as a float value between 0 and 100. You can only specify percentiles or bounds, not both.

upper_percentile.title: Upper Percentile
upper_percentile.description: Specify a custom upper-bound percentile for rescaling as a float value between 0 and 100. You can only specify percentiles or bounds, not both.

lower_bound.title: Lower Bound
lower_bound.description: Explicit lower bound value to rescale the image at. Needs to be an integer, e.g. 100. You can only specify percentiles or bounds, not both.

upper_bound.title: Upper Bound
upper_bound.description: Explicit upper bound value to rescale the image at. Needs to be an integer, e.g. 2000. You can only specify percentiles or bounds, not both.

histogram_name.title: Histogram Name
histogram_name.description: Name of the histogram to use for rescaling in `histogram` mode. Needs to be a string, e.g. "channel_histograms".

CustomNormalizer.title: CustomNormalizer

wavelength_id.title: Wavelength Id
wavelength_id.description: Unique ID for the channel wavelength, e.g. `A01_C01`. Can only be specified if label is not set.

label.title: Label
label.description: Name of the channel. Can only be specified if wavelength_id is not set.

normalize.title: Normalize
normalize.description: Set image normalization method and parameters.

zarr_url.title: Zarr Url
zarr_url.description: Path or url to the individual OME-Zarr image to be processed. (standard argument for Fractal tasks, managed by Fractal server).

pyramid_level.title: Pyramid Level
pyramid_level.description: Pyramid level of the image to be segmented. Choose `0` to process at full resolution.

channel.title: Channel
channel.description: Channel for segmentation; requires either `wavelength_id` (e.g. `A01_C01`) or `label` (e.g. `DAPI`), but not both. Also contains normalization options.

input_ROI_table.title: Input Roi Table
input_ROI_table.description: Name of the ROI table over which the task loops to apply segmentation. Examples: `FOV_ROI_table` => loop over the field of views, `organoid_ROI_table` => loop over the organoid ROI table (generated by another task), `well_ROI_table` => process the whole well as one image.

output_ROI_table.title: Output Roi Table
output_ROI_table.description: If provided, a ROI table with that name is created, which will contain the bounding boxes of the newly segmented labels. ROI tables should have `ROI` in their name.

output_label_name.title: Output Label Name
output_label_name.description: Name of the output label image (e.g. `"organoids"`).

gpu.title: Gpu
gpu.description: If `True`, use the GPU for segmentation.

model_type.title: Model Type
model_type.description: Type of cellpose model to use for segmentation.

diameter.title: Diameter
diameter.description: Diameter of the objects to be segmented (pixels at level 0).

resample.title: Resample
resample.description: Run dynamics at original image size (will be slower but create more accurate boundaries).

overwrite_existing_label.title: Overwrite Existing Label
overwrite_existing_label.description: If `True`, overwrite the created labels, if they already exist.

task_list.docs_info:
## segment_cellpose_simple
Segment a single channel using cellpose.

CustomNormalizer.description: Validator to handle different image normalization scenarios.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: Normalization mode. 'default': Data is normalized so 0.0=1st percentile and 1.0=99th percentile of image intensities. 'no_normalization': No normalization is applied. 'custom': You can either provide your own rescaling percentiles or fixed rescaling upper and lower bound integers. 'omero': The "start" and "end" values from the omero channels in the zarr file are used for upper and lower bounds. 'histogram': A precalculated histogram is used for normalization. Percentiles need to be provided.

lower_percentile.title: Lower Percentile
lower_percentile.description: Specify a custom lower-bound percentile for rescaling as a float value between 0 and 100. You can only specify percentiles or bounds, not both.

upper_percentile.title: Upper Percentile
upper_percentile.description: Specify a custom upper-bound percentile for rescaling as a float value between 0 and 100. You can only specify percentiles or bounds, not both.

lower_bound.title: Lower Bound
lower_bound.description: Explicit lower bound value to rescale the image at. Needs to be an integer, e.g. 100. You can only specify percentiles or bounds, not both.

histogram_name.title: Histogram Name

CustomNormalizer.title: CustomNormalizer

wavelength_id.title: Wavelength Id
wavelength_id.description: Unique ID for the channel wavelength, e.g. `A01_C01`. Can only be specified if label is not set.

label.title: Label
label.description: Name of the channel. Can only be specified if wavelength_id is not set.

normalize.title: Normalize
normalize.description: Set image normalization method and parameters.

zarr_url.title: Zarr Url
zarr_url.description: Path or url to the individual OME-Zarr image to be processed. (standard argument for Fractal tasks, managed by Fractal server).

pyramid_level.title: Pyramid Level
pyramid_level.description: Pyramid level of the image to be segmented. Choose `0` to process at full resolution.

channel.title: Channel
channel.description: Channel for segmentation; requires either `wavelength_id` (e.g. `A01_C01`) or `label` (e.g. `DAPI`), but not both. Also contains normalization options: 'default': Data is normalized so 0.0=1st percentile and 1.0=99th percentile of image intensities. 'no_normalization': No normalization is applied. 'custom': You can either provide your own rescaling percentiles or fixed rescaling upper and lower bound integers. 'omero': The "start" and "end" values from the omero channels in the zarr file are used for upper and lower bounds. 'histogram': A precalculated histogram is used for normalization. Percentiles need to be provided.

input_ROI_table.title: Input Roi Table
input_ROI_table.description: Name of the ROI table over which the task loops to apply segmentation. Examples: `FOV_ROI_table` => loop over the field of views, `organoid_ROI_table` => loop over the organoid ROI table (generated by another task), `well_ROI_table` => process the whole well as one image.

output_ROI_table.title: Output Roi Table
output_ROI_table.description: If provided, a ROI table with that name is created, which will contain the bounding boxes of the newly segmented labels. ROI tables should have `ROI` in their name.

output_label_name.title: Output Label Name
output_label_name.description: Name of the output label image (e.g. `"organoids"`).

gaussian_smoothing_sigma.title: Gaussian Smoothing Sigma
gaussian_smoothing_sigma.description: sigma for preprocessing gaussian filter (in pixels @ level0)

s2_param.title: S2 Param
s2_param.description: list of list of parameters for segmentation. The first element is the sigma for the laplacian of gaussian, and the second element is the threshold for the filtered image: [[sigma1, threshold1], [sigma2, threshold2], ...] e.g. [[1, 0.04], [1.5, 0.8], [2, 0.15], [4, 0.20]] (sigma in pixels @ chosen level)

fill_2d.title: Fill 2D
fill_2d.description: If True, holes will be filled

fill_max_size.title: Fill Max Size
fill_max_size.description: maximum hole-size to be filled (in pixels @ level0)

task_list.docs_info:
## segment_particles
Segment spot-like particles in 2D image.

Function inspired by the Allen Cell & Structure Segmenter. The steps are:
- Normalization (limits taken from OMERO-channel metadata)
- Gaussian smoothing (gaussian_smoothing_sigma)
- Laplacian of Gaussian filtering & thresholding at multiple scales (s2_param)
- Hole filling (fill_2d, fill_max_size)
- Seeded watershed segmentation, using the local maxima of the smoothed image
  (gaussian_smoothing_sigma) as seeds.

task_list.name: SMO background estimation

zarr_url.title: Zarr Url
zarr_url.description: Absolute path to the OME-Zarr image. (standard argument for Fractal tasks, managed by Fractal server).

sigma.title: Sigma
sigma.description: Standard deviation for Gaussian pre-filter to reduce noise.

size.title: Size
size.description: Window size in pixels to average gradient. Should be smaller than foreground objects & background regions.

subtract_background.title: Subtract Background
subtract_background.description: If True, subtract the estimated background from the image (clipping at zero).

overwrite_input_image.title: Overwrite Input Image
overwrite_input_image.description: If True, overwrite the input image. If False, create a new well sub-group to store the corrected image. Only used if subtract_background is True.

new_well_subgroup_suffix.title: New Well Subgroup Suffix
new_well_subgroup_suffix.description: Suffix to add to the new well sub-group name. Only used if overwrite_input_image is False.

task_list.docs_info:
## smo_background_estimation
Estimates background of each FOV using SMO.

Uses the SMO algorithm to estimate background for each FOV & channel. In
short, SMO uses local gradient amount to identify background pixels. See
the SMO publication for details: https://doi.org/10.1364/JOSAA.477468

Limitation: Currently, does not support 3D or time-lapse images.
